headline: Iran Strike Rallies Boosting Tech Monday
detail: Data shows weekend Iran strikes have repeatedly triggered Monday risk-on rallies. This lifts your tech-heavy book short-term — SMH, CRWD, QQQ all benefit from growth appetite. But these moves are sentiment-driven and can reverse quickly, which matters a lot on margin.

headline: Rocket Lab Challenges SpaceX with $8B Acquisition
detail: Rocket Lab is making a major move into satellite internet to compete with Starlink. This is directly relevant to your SPCX and SPAL positions — more competition in the SpaceX lane is a headwind for valuation sentiment on a stock that's already a brand-new IPO with thin data.

headline: Tesla Self-Driving Update Sparks Stock Rally
detail: Tesla's big move on autonomy news signals the market is rewarding AI-driven tech stories today. That's a broadly positive read for your tech/chip names like AVGO, PLTR, and SMH, confirming the risk-on tone is real, at least for today.

headline: Supreme Court Lets Presidents Fire Independent Regulators
detail: The FTC ruling shifts regulatory power toward the White House, reducing independent oversight of big tech M&A and antitrust actions. For your chip and software names, lighter regulatory friction on deals is a mild long-term positive.
